page: Microsoft Azure D128ads_v7 is a Dadsv7-series virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor using the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 7559 GB of local disk capacity and supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are bundled with this server type.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as measured by stress-ng and PassMark. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 12266.71 GB/sec and a large-block uncached read bandwidth of 389.75 GB/sec. In database and key-value store evaluations, the server achieves 177.38 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and scores in the top tier for PassMark database operations. Large-scale data processing is supported by high decompression throughput, reaching 875.75 GB/sec in multi-threaded bzip3 tests.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-capacity local storage, the D128ads_v7 provides high resource density for demanding enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale relational databases, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data compression tasks. Additionally, the CPU's support for advanced vector instructions enables efficient execution of local LLM inference, with prompt processing speeds reaching 330.81 tokens per second on medium 7B models. The absence of a dedicated GPU represents a tradeoff for deep learning training, but the high core count and memory bandwidth offer a cost-efficient alternative for CPU-bound analytical and inference workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D128ads_v7 is a Dadsv7-series virtual machine powered by a 64-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, delivering 128 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 7559 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance lacks a dedicated GPU but delivers top-tier performance across CPU and memory benchmarks. It achieves top-10% rankings in multi-core stress-ng tests, Redis SET operations at 177.38 million operations per second, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 875.75 GB/sec. This server is optimized for high-throughput database operations, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128ads_v7 is a 128-vCPU virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 512 GB of RAM, and 7559 GB of local storage, optimized for high-throughput database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128ads_v7 features 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-threaded performance.
tagline: A high-core AMD EPYC virtual machine with balanced memory and massive local storage for demanding database and data processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 128 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM and 7559 GB of local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Excellent throughput for Redis and database operations
Well-suited for CPU-based LLM inference and web serving
